items.content: Finally, we’ve been talking to lots of users about helping them make the most of Claude Code. We’ll be sharing more this week on that work, starting with our updates to /usage.

items.title: You can also set your autocompact threshold yourself and effectively lower your context window…
items.content: You can also set your autocompact threshold yourself and effectively lower your context window if you'd prefer. For example, 400k context is a good compromise: CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW=400000 claude see docs here: https://t.co/NuhBuTSSDt
items.summary: You can also set your autocompact threshold yourself and effectively lower your context window if you'd prefer. For example, 400k context is a good compromise: CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW=400000 claude see docs here…

items.title: I edited the intro because I realized I buried the lede originally- The 1M context window is a…
items.content: I edited the intro because I realized I buried the lede originally- The 1M context window is a double-edged sword. It allows Claude to do more complex tasks but it can also leads to more context pollution if you don't manage your session well. This is how you do that: https://t.co/nVWS6cIwO2

items.title: A few questions on plan vs ultraplan. I think the insight here is that implementation can somet…
items.content: A few questions on plan vs ultraplan. I think the insight here is that implementation can sometimes require a local environment/interactivity, but planning can happen in the cloud since it's mostly about reading code & understanding intent.

items.title: New in Claude Code: /ultraplan Claude builds an implementation plan for you on the web. You can…
items.content: New in Claude Code: /ultraplan Claude builds an implementation plan for you on the web. You can read it and edit it, then run the plan on the web or back in your terminal. Available now in preview for all users with CC on the web enabled.
items.summary: New in Claude Code: /ultraplan Claude builds an implementation plan for you on the web. You can read it and edit it, then run the plan on the web or back in your terminal. Available now in preview for all users with CC…

items.title: you'll need to explicitly prompt Claude Code to use it, but the Monitor Tool is super powerful…
items.content: you'll need to explicitly prompt Claude Code to use it, but the Monitor Tool is super powerful e.g. "start my dev server and use the MonitorTool to observe for errors" https://t.co/xVUr8MysOi
items.summary: you'll need to explicitly prompt Claude Code to use it, but the Monitor Tool is super powerful e.g. "start my dev server and use the MonitorTool to observe for errors" https://t.co/xVUr8MysOi

items.title: I think "prompting" will keep being an incredibly high-leverage skill, like writing or public s…
items.content: I think "prompting" will keep being an incredibly high-leverage skill, like writing or public speaking. It is the skill of talking to agents, mediated by the harness. My main goal is to grow the bandwidth between humans and agents, to help us understand each other better.
items.summary: I think "prompting" will keep being an incredibly high-leverage skill, like writing or public speaking. It is the skill of talking to agents, mediated by the harness. My main goal is to grow the bandwidth between humans…

items.content: I want to do some streams where I work with non-technical people using Claude Code to figure out how they might be able to improve their process. My feeling is that just a few tips could make a big difference in efficiency. Any mutuals interested?
